Walking Calorie Burn Calculator (Australia)

Walking is one of the most effective and sustainable physical activities for long-term health and weight loss. Use our walking calorie burn calculator to calculate how many calories you burn based on your body weight, duration, and walking pace.

lbs
min

Calculation Results

Estimated Burn -- kcal
Metabolic Equivalent (MET) --
--

How Walking Calories Are Calculated

Exercise scientists measure physical activity intensity in **Metabolic Equivalents (METs)**. One MET represents the rate at which your body expends energy while resting. Walking at a moderate speed of 3.0 mph has a MET value of 3.5. This means you burn 3.5 times more energy than sitting still.

The clinical formula to determine calorie burn is:

Calories = (MET × 3.5 × Body Weight in kg) ÷ 200 × Duration in Minutes

Step and Deficit Strategy

Integrating walking with a dietary deficit is the most sustainable approach to fat loss. For instance, walking 10,000 steps covers roughly 4-5 miles, burning between 300 to 500 calories depending on your body weight. Combining this active expenditure with a moderate deficit established using our calorie deficit calculator can double your fat loss speed. For more details on the metabolic impact of walking, check our guide on walking for weight loss steps and calorie metrics.

Scientific Citation: The metabolic cost of transport determines active caloric expenditure. Check exercise physiology studies on walking energy costs at Wikipedia's Intermittent Fasting & Exercise Page.
